notes: DDR5-6400 stayed anchored by 32GB/64GB/128GB tracked values across the review window, with 96GB repaired but still not central to tracking and 16GB still absent.
DDR5-5600 remained structurally split at 32GB for the full review window; this is persistently outlier_only rather than no_public_sample.
PCIe4 TLC remained the steadiest and most executable category over the last 7-14 days, with public distribution coverage holding after the 30.72TB recovery.
PCIe5 TLC kept decent nominal coverage, but the key unresolved problem remains 30.72TB anchor decay: the old lower-band reference has not returned cleanly since 2026-04-13.

PCIe4 QLC stayed fully covered, but supplementation still depends partly on classifying Mouser, PCNation and Wiredzone correctly as parser-gap or RFQ sources instead of treating them as true no-price evidence.

keyChangesVsYesterday: No tracked representative value changed versus 2026-04-17; the market still looks like stable main bands plus unresolved structural splits.
DDR5-5600 32GB was rechecked and remains clearly outlier_only rather than no_public_sample: low retail pages and high distributor pages both remain visible.
PCIe5 TLC 30.72TB still failed to recover the older lower-band anchor cleanly, so stale_repeated_sample remains the right explanation.

Mouser search-side evidence again suggests a visible QLC price exists, but direct extraction remains unreliable, so parser_gap is still the correct classification rather than no public market pricing.

DDR5-6400.reason: 64GB and 128GB remain stable, but 32GB still lacks a stronger clean exact public anchor and 16GB is still absent.

DDR5-5600.reason: 32GB remains structurally split across low retail and high distributor bands, while 64GB and 128GB remain visible but are not at attractive low levels.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: This remains the most stable and fully covered category, with repeatable public distribution bands and no new sign of anchor decay.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: Lower capacities remain observable, but 30.72TB still lacks a trustworthy single representative public band because the old lower anchor remains stale while reproducible Micron high-band quotes persist.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Main premium public bands remain usable, but high-capacity low clusters are still too noisy to justify a one-shot bottom-call.

gaps.note: Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.

Low-side public pages remain far below the higher distributor band, so the slot still cannot support a single representative tracking value.
The previously preferred lower-band anchor still did not repeat cleanly; current evidence remains split between a sold-out low cluster and reproducible Micron high-band quotes.
Search-side pricing remains visible, but the direct page still is not reliably extractable in today's workflow, so this stays a parser problem rather than a no-price problem.
